data.title: Medline to participate in Barclays 28th Annual Global Healthcare Conference in Miami Beach on March 11, 2026 at 9:30am ET.
data.description: In Northfield, Illinois, on February 18, 2026, Medline Inc. revealed that its CEO, Jim Boyle, along with CFO Mike Drazin, will be speaking at the Barclays 28th Global Healthcare Conference in Miami Beach, Florida. The presentation is set for Wednesday, March 11, 2026, at 9:30am Eastern Time. Medline is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol MDLN.

data.title: Creative Realities repurchases Slipstream warrants, reducing dilution exposure and improving visibility for shareholders.
data.description: Decreases dilution exposure in the interest of shareholders by improving visibility. This move aims to safeguard shareholder value and enhance transparency in the company's operations.

data.title: Apollo Silver is ranked 13th in the 2026 TSX Venture 50, highlighting the company as one of the top performers on the TSX Venture Exchange.
data.description: A company has been acknowledged as one of the top-performing issuers on the TSX Venture Exchange. The company's exceptional performance has placed it among the standout performers on this prominent stock exchange. This recognition highlights the company's success and growth in the market, indicating its strong performance compared to its peers. The achievement on the TSX Venture Exchange underscores the company's position as a leading player in the financial landscape. This acknowledgment serves as a testament to the company's impressive standing and signifies its notable accomplishments on the exchange.

data.title: Medicenna Therapeutics to participate in conferences to showcase developments; focus on Superkines targeting diseases.
data.description: On February 18, 2026, Toronto and Houston will host Medicenna Therapeutics Corp. ("Medicenna" or "the Company"), a clinical-stage immunotherapy firm concentrating on Superkines that target cancer, autoimmune, and inflammatory illnesses. The company will be participating in multiple forthcoming conferences, including those showcasing its recent advancements across different programs. These events aim to facilitate interaction with the industry and investment sector. Medicenna's stock is listed on TSX under the ticker symbol MDNA and on OTCQX as MDNAF.

data.title: Crescent Biopharma starts ASCEND trial dosing 1st patient with CR-001, bispecific antibody, for solid tumors incl NSCLC, GI, gynecological.
data.description: ASCEND will conduct a clinical trial to assess CR-001, a bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and VEGF, in various solid tumor types. The study will involve patients with non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), as well as gastrointestinal and gynecological cancers. Both first-line and previously treated patients will be included in the trial. This trial aims to evaluate the efficacy of CR-001 in treating these cancers and will enroll a significant number of participants across different tumor types.

data.title: NervGen Pharma announces participation in investor conferences, CEO Adam Rogers to attend. TSXV: NGEN, NASDAQ: NGEN.
data.description: NervGen Pharma Corp., a biopharmaceutical company based in Vancouver, British Columbia, revealed today that its President and CEO, Adam Rogers, MD, along with other executives, will be attending various investor conferences in the near future. The company, listed on both TSXV (under the ticker NGEN) and NASDAQ (under the same ticker symbol), specializes in developing innovative treatments for spinal cord injuries and other neurotraumatic conditions. This announcement was made on February 18, 2026.

data.title: Nexxen will report Q4 2025 financial results on March 4, 2026. Expert in data and Advanced TV. NASDAQ: NEXN.
data.description: On February 18, 2026, Nexxen International Ltd. (NASDAQ: NEXN) announced that they will unveil their financial report for the three and twelve months concluding on December 31, 2025, before the U.S. market commences on Wednesday, March 4, 2026. Nexxen is a worldwide advertising technology platform known for its proficiency in data and advanced TV services. Investors and stakeholders are eager to examine the company's performance during this period.

data.title: Cadence Design Systems beats Q4 estimates with $1.44B revenue, $1.99 EPS. Raised Q1 outlook to $1.89-$1.95 EPS. CEO optimistic on AI-driven products.
data.description: Cadence Design Systems Inc (NASDAQ: CDNS) released their fourth-quarter financial results, surpassing expectations. With a revenue of $1.44 billion, higher than the projected $1.42 billion, and adjusted earnings per share of $1.99, exceeding the estimated $1.91 per share. Anirudh Devgan, the company's president and CEO, attributed the success to robust customer demand for their AI-driven products and the importance of Cadence's engineering software in today's market. Looking ahead, Cadence Design Systems anticipates first-quarter adjusted earnings to fall within $1.89 to $1.95 per share, notably higher than the estimated $1.80 per share. The company's optimistic outlook is driven by the substantial opportunities present in the AI era. For further details, refer to the complete article available on Benzinga.com.
Cerrado Gold ranked among top 50 on TSX Venture Exchange.

data.title: Streamex launching GLDY, gold-backed tokenized security on Feb 25, 2026, promising institutional-grade yield.
data.description: On February 25, 2026, GLDY is set to release a new product - an institutional-grade asset backed by gold that will provide significant yield opportunities. This initiative marks a major step in the company's strategy to cater to institutional investors and create a more diversified portfolio. By launching this product, GLDY is aiming to offer a unique investment opportunity that combines the stability of gold with attractive yield potential. This move is expected to attract a significant number of investors looking to capitalize on this innovative offering.

data.title: Amazon scraps Blue Jay warehouse robot after just six months due to high costs and complexity, pivots to new tech like Flex Cell.
data.description: Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN) has halted the use of its warehouse robot, Blue Jay, which was launched in October but discontinued in January. The robot, aimed at same-day delivery warehouses, encountered challenges including high costs and manufacturing complexities. Staff working on Blue Jay have been reassigned to other robotics projects within the company. Despite the setback, Amazon plans to integrate Blue Jay's technology into upcoming systems, with a new project called "Flex Cell" in the works. This decision is part of Amazon's transition from the previous same-day warehouse model, known as "Local Vending Machine" (LVM), to a more versatile system called "Orbital." Unlike LVM, Orbital is modular and comprises multiple components.

data.title: Verisk Analytics beats Q4 earnings estimates with EPS at $1.82 (14.47% above estimate) and revenue up by $43.20 million.
data.description: Verisk Analytics (NASDAQ: VRSK) reported its Q4 earnings on February 18, 2026, at 07:15 AM. The company exceeded earnings expectations by 14.47%, posting an EPS of $1.82 compared to the estimated $1.59. Revenue saw an increase of $43.20 million from the previous year. Additional details can be found in the full story at Benzinga.com.

data.title: Genesis Energy, L.P. (NYSE: GEL) filed its Form 10-K for fiscal year ended December 31, 2025 with SEC.
data.description: Genesis Energy, L.P. (NYSE: GEL) based in Houston has released its Annual Report for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2025, which has been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission. The Form 10-K is available for access on the company's website at https://www.genesisenergy.com/investors/sec-filings. Those interested can also request a physical copy of the report at no cost by sending an email to InvestorRelations@genlp.com. Genesis Energy, L.P. is a versatile midstream energy master.

data.title: ParaZero intercepts hostile drones at 70 mph in successful internal test. Company Nasdaq: PRZO is leading aerospace innovator.
data.description: Israeli company ParaZero Technologies Ltd. (Nasdaq: PRZO) recently conducted successful live testing of its DefendAir™ system, which offers smart, autonomous solutions for both manned and unmanned aerial systems (UAS) worldwide. The internal testing revealed exceptional results, showcasing the effectiveness of the defense technology. The company is positioned as a leader in the aerospace defense sector with its innovative approach.

data.title: Mill Creek Residential breaks ground on Modera Allston in Boston, adding 240 homes to the neighborhood.
data.description: Major developer Mill Creek Residential has revealed plans to construct 240 new homes in a modern midrise community in the western Boston neighborhood. The development, expected to significantly impact the area, is set to be completed by 2026. Mill Creek Residential is renowned for its expertise in developing and managing high-quality rental housing properties throughout the United States.

data.title: Bain & Co. appoints Tom de Waele as new regional managing partner for EMEA, effective January 26.
data.description: In London on February 18, 2026, Bain & Company revealed the appointment of Tom De Waele as the new regional managing partner for Europe, the Middle East & Africa (EMEA) starting from January 26. De Waele steps into the role during a pivotal period for the firm in EMEA, where key strategies and initiatives are being implemented.

data.title: Charles River (NYSE: CRL) Q4 earnings on 2/18/26 beat estimates by 2.14%, EPS $2.39 vs $2.34, revenue down $8.77M.
data.description: On Wednesday, February 18, 2026 at 07:00 AM, Charles River (NYSE: CRL) released its Q4 earnings report. The company surpassed estimated earnings by 2.14%, with an EPS of $2.39 compared to the estimated $2.34. Revenue decreased by $8.77 million from the corresponding period in the previous year. Looking back at the historical earnings summary, in the last quarter, the company exceeded expectations... For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Verisk's Q4 2025 revenue was $779M, up 5.9%, net income $197M, adjusted EBITDA $437M. Full-year revenue $3.1B, net income $908M.
data.description: Verisk, a strategic data analytics partner, reported its Q4 and full-year 2025 results. In Q4, revenue was $779M (up 5.9%), net income $197M (down 6.2%), and adjusted EBITDA $437M (up 9.8%). Diluted GAAP EPS was $1.42 (down 1.4%), and diluted adjusted EPS was $1.82 (up 13.0%). Full-year 2025 revenue was $3,073M (up 6.6%), net income was $908M (down 5.1%), and adjusted EBITDA was $1,727M (up 9.6%). The Board approved a cash dividend increase to 50 cents per share and a share repurchase authorization to $2.5B. Q4 net cash provided by operating activities was $343M (up 34.4%), and free cash flow was $276M (up 38.0%). In 2026, the company expects revenue of $3,190-3,240M, adjusted EBITDA of $1,790-1,830M, and diluted adjusted EPS of $7.45-7.75. Verisk terminated an acquisition agreement and sold a business unit in Q4 2025, impacting revenues. On February 13, 2026, the Board approved a 50 cents per share cash dividend and a share repurchase program. Verisk emphasized the importance of non-GAAP financial measures, including adjusted EBITDA, diluted adjusted EPS, and free cash flow, and highlighted the impact of foreign currency on its operations.
